text: Entering this space, I am immediately aware of its stark modernity—the sharp lines, glass reflections, and orderly utilitarian features. Crossing the barrier between outside and in, I anticipate some threshold of meaning or revelation just beyond mundane reality. As I step closer to the R+D display, I feel a growing curiosity tinged with self-consciousness—my actions and thoughts reflected in the inventions and intentions this place archives. The display's words seem to echo my own feelings: doubt, exposure, longing for understanding. There is comfort in knowing my awkwardness is not singular but part of the fabric of being observed and observing here. I feel both small and included, wondering what it means to be understood—not just seen—within these creative, analytical walls.
